tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P1] Factor a shared binary resolver and resolve Codex's SDK-vendored binary
tasks.description:
Fix the T0 bug: Codex's binary is vendored by @openai/codex-sdk yet `resolveCodexBinaryPath` in `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/check-dependencies.ts` only probes PATH, so Codex falsely reports 'needs setup'. Two deliverables (ADR-0316):

(a) NEW generic resolver `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/shared/resolve-binary.ts` exporting `resolveRuntimeBinary(...)` that returns the first existing candidate under the precedence 'SDK-vendored -> configured binaryPath -> PATH'. It takes candidate producers (functions returning `string | null`), existence-checks each, and falls through when a source's path does not exist. It must NOT import any runtime SDK (it stays generic so ESLint's per-adapter SDK confinement never applies).

(b) In `codex/check-dependencies.ts`, add `resolveCodexVendoredBinary()` mirroring `resolveBundledClaudeBinary()` in `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/sdk/sdk-utils.ts`: use `createRequire(import.meta.url).resolve(...)` against the `@openai/codex` optional platform package to locate the vendored executable at `@openai/codex/vendor/<triple>/bin/codex` (verified layout: `aarch64-apple-darwin` on darwin-arm64 per codex/NOTES.md). Map `process.platform`/`process.arch` to the Rust target triple. This is a `require.resolve` STRING, not an `@openai/codex-sdk` import, so it stays within the codex adapter's ESLint boundary. Rewire `resolveCodexBinaryPath()` to compose the shared resolver with [vendored candidate, configured `runtimes.codex.binaryPath`, `findCodexOnPath()`].

DECISION FLAG (reversible): ADR-0316 states precedence 'vendored -> configured -> PATH', but today a configured `binaryPath` is authoritative (missing -> report missing, never fall through). Default to ADR-0316's order (vendored first); if review prefers an explicit override to win, flip to 'configured -> vendored -> PATH'. Preserve the existing honesty that a configured-but-absent path surfaces as a resolution miss rather than silently probing PATH.

Acceptance criteria with concrete scenarios (Vitest, tests in `codex/__tests__/check-dependencies.test.ts` + new `shared/__tests__/resolve-binary.test.ts`):
- With NO `codex` on PATH and no configured path, `resolveCodexBinaryPath()` returns the vendored path and `checkCodexDependencies()` reports the 'Codex CLI' check `satisfied` (mock the vendored file existing + `runCodex('--version')` succeeding) — the false-'needs setup' bug is gone.
- Vendored file absent but PATH has `codex` -> resolver falls through to the PATH result.
- All sources absent -> returns null and the CLI check is `missing`.
- `resolveRuntimeBinary` returns the FIRST existing candidate and skips any candidate whose path fails the existence check.
- Keep the Codex `runtimeConformance` suite green; TSDoc on all new exports.
tasks.activeForm: Resolving the Codex SDK-vendored binary and factoring the shared resolver

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P1] Make dependency probes async and time-bounded (absorb DOR-180 follow-up)
tasks.description:
The `AgentRuntime.checkDependencies(): Promise<DependencyCheck[]>` interface (`packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts:474`) is already Promise-typed and `GET /api/system/requirements` already `await`s it — but the Codex and OpenCode adapters run synchronous `execFileSync` / `which` / `existsSync` probes internally, so a hung `which` (e.g. a PATH entry on a stalled network mount) or a slow CLI blocks the Node event loop and stutters live SSE streams. This absorbs the DOR-180 follow-up 'make checkDependencies probes async'.

Convert the internal probes to non-blocking in `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/check-dependencies.ts` and `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/check-dependencies.ts`: replace `execFileSync` with `promisify(execFile)` bounded by the existing `PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS` (5s) plus a `killSignal`, so a hung `--version` / `login status` / `auth list` / `which` rejects fast and degrades to `missing` rather than hanging. Make `resolveCodexBinaryPath`, `resolveOpenCodeBinaryPath`, `checkCodexDependencies`, `checkOpenCodeDependencies`, and the shared `resolveRuntimeBinary` (from 1.1) async where they shell out. Update `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/server-manager.ts`'s call to `resolveOpenCodeBinaryPath()` on sidecar boot to `await` it. Do NOT change the `checkDependencies` return type — it is already a Promise, so the conformance suite signature is unaffected.

Acceptance criteria with concrete scenarios (Vitest):
- A probe that never resolves (mock `execFile` to hang) rejects/degrades within the bounded window and yields a `missing` check — assert the test resolves (use fake timers) rather than hanging.
- Existing `check-dependencies.test.ts` cases updated to `await` and still pass.
- `pnpm typecheck` clean; both runtimes' `runtimeConformance` suites stay green.
- A satisfied binary still reports its version (async path returns the same trimmed stdout as the old sync path).
tasks.activeForm: Making the dependency probes async and bounded

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P1] Project per-runtime Ready/Connect state in the requirements API
tasks.description:
Add the two-state Ready/Connect model to the server truth surface so the three runtimes can present as siblings. In `packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts`, extend `SystemRequirements['runtimes'][type]` (currently `{ dependencies: DependencyCheck[] }`) with a derived `state: 'ready' | 'connect'` and an optional `connect?: { kind: 'install' | 'login' | 'provider-picker'; label: string }`. Keep the raw `dependencies[]` (the client Advanced disclosure consumes it) and keep `allSatisfied`.

In `apps/server/src/routes/system.ts`, compute the projection from each runtime's `DependencyCheck[]` after `await runtime.checkDependencies()`: `state = 'ready'` iff the CLI-binary check is `satisfied` AND (the auth check is `satisfied` OR the runtime declares auth not required, e.g. a detected local model); otherwise `'connect'`. Derive `connect.kind`: a `missing` binary -> `install`; a satisfied binary with `missing` auth -> `login`; OpenCode's provider-agnostic auth -> `provider-picker` (a coarse default for T0; T1 enriches). Set an honest `label` (never a raw dependency error string).

Acceptance criteria with concrete scenarios (Vitest, new `apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/system.test.ts`, mocking `runtimeRegistry.listRuntimes()`):
- Binary + auth satisfied -> `state:'ready'`, no `connect`.
- Binary missing -> `state:'connect'`, `connect.kind:'install'`.
- Binary present + auth missing -> `state:'connect'`, `connect.kind:'login'`.
- A slow/hung probe (from 1.2) does not block the handler — assert the route still responds within a bounded window.
- The raw `dependencies[]` remain present in the payload for every runtime.
tasks.activeForm: Projecting per-runtime Ready/Connect state in the requirements API

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P1] Reframe the client setup surface to Ready/Connect with an Advanced disclosure
tasks.description:
Reframe the client setup UI from raw dependency rows + copy-this-command blocks to the two-state Ready/Connect model, presenting all three runtimes identically as siblings, with binary/CLI/sidecar detail hidden behind an Advanced disclosure (Apple Test: describe what happens for the user; keep internals for the Priya persona).

Files (client, entities/runtime + features/status; FSD: entities may not import features/widgets):
- `apps/client/src/layers/entities/runtime/model/use-runtime-requirements.ts`: surface the new server `state`/`connect` projection (from 1.3) alongside the existing `isRuntimeReady` helper.
- `apps/client/src/layers/entities/runtime/ui/RuntimeSetupDialog.tsx` (`RuntimeSetupPanel` / `RuntimeSection` / `DependencyRow`): each runtime renders either 'Ready' (green check) OR a single 'Connect' call-to-action built from `connect.kind` + `connect.label` — never a raw dependency error and never a copy-command block on the default path. Move the per-dependency rows and `DependencyInstallHint` command block into an `<Advanced>` collapsible (shadcn Collapsible/Accordion), collapsed by default.
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/status/ui/RuntimeItem.tsx`: the picker's 'needs setup' entries become 'Connect' entries opening the same Ready/Connect surface; all addable runtimes render the same shape.
- Update dialog copy so it no longer says 'Run these steps in your terminal' for connectable runtimes.

Three-siblings requirement: in the 'Add a runtime'/overview mode, Claude, Codex, and OpenCode each appear as Ready-or-one-Connect using one shared component — no runtime shows a structurally different surface.

Acceptance criteria (RTL + jsdom, mock Transport via `TransportProvider`, in `entities/runtime/__tests__/RuntimeSetupDialog.test.tsx` and a `RuntimeItem` test):
- A runtime with `state:'ready'` shows Ready and NO Connect button.
- `state:'connect'` shows exactly one Connect CTA with the server `label`.
- The binary/CLI vocabulary is absent from the default DOM and appears only after expanding Advanced.
- All three runtimes render the same component shape (assert identical structure across Claude/Codex/OpenCode).
- No FSD import-direction lint errors; existing entity tests updated.
tasks.activeForm: Reframing the client setup surface to Ready/Connect with an Advanced disclosure

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P1] Add opt-in, on-demand OpenCode provisioning (server action + endpoint)
tasks.description:
Make OpenCode installable in one click without a terminal, without bundling it into the base install (ADR-0317). Because `opencode-ai@1.17.13` declares 12 os/cpu-gated `optionalDependencies`, installing the package pulls ONLY the current platform's binary — a clean on-demand vendoring path replacing `npm i -g opencode-ai`.

Deliverables (server):
- NEW `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/provision.ts`: installs `opencode-ai` (version-matched to the `@opencode-ai/sdk` already in `apps/server/package.json`) into a DorkOS-owned location resolved via `lib/dork-home.ts` — NEVER `os.homedir()` (hard rule) — e.g. under `{dorkHome}/runtimes/opencode/`. Spawn a scoped installer (`npm install --prefix <dir> opencode-ai@<pinned>` or equivalent) and stream progress. On success, make `resolveOpenCodeBinaryPath` (via the shared resolver from 1.1, adding a provisioned-location candidate) resolve the provisioned binary so OpenCode flips to Ready. Handle partial/failed installs: remove the partial tree and resolve back to a single Connect action with an honest message (mirror the transaction cleanup ethos, though this is a package install, not the marketplace transaction).
- Endpoint `POST /api/system/runtimes/opencode/provision` in `apps/server/src/routes/system.ts` (or a new `apps/server/src/routes/runtimes.ts` mounted in `apps/server/src/app.ts` next to the other `app.use('/api/...')` lines) that triggers the install and reports progress (SSE stream or a trigger + poll-status pair), loopback-only.
- Transport: add the method to `packages/shared/src/transport.ts` and implement in both `HttpTransport` and `DirectTransport` adapters (mirror how `checkRequirements`/`getCapabilities` are wired).

DECISION FLAG (reversible): install location + package-manager invocation (dork-home-scoped `npm install --prefix` vs a `pnpm add` into a scoped dir). Default: dork-home-scoped `npm install --prefix` to stay package-manager-agnostic on the user's machine and keep the base install lean. Flag for review.

Acceptance criteria (Vitest integration, MOCK the spawned installer — never run real `npm install` in CI):
- Success path: install completes, the subsequent requirements probe (1.3) flips OpenCode to Ready.
- Failure path (installer non-zero exit): partial tree cleaned up, no binary left resolvable, endpoint returns a Connect/error state with an honest message.
- The endpoint rejects non-loopback origins.
tasks.activeForm: Adding opt-in on-demand OpenCode provisioning

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P1] Wire the OpenCode Connect CTA to provisioning with inline progress
tasks.description:
Connect the client's OpenCode 'Connect' CTA (from 1.4) to the provisioning endpoint (1.5) so a Claude-only user installs OpenCode in one click. When OpenCode's server `state` is `connect` with `connect.kind:'install'`, the Connect action calls the provision transport method, shows inline progress (a `motion`-driven progress row per `contributing/animations.md`), and on success invalidates the `['requirements']` TanStack Query key so OpenCode flips to Ready WITHOUT a manual 'Check again'. On failure, surface the honest error and return to a single retryable Connect action (Advanced disclosure still exposes the underlying detail).

Files (client): extend the entities/runtime setup UI, or introduce a small feature slice `apps/client/src/layers/features/runtime-connect/` (FSD: features may import entities/shared). Use a TanStack Query mutation + `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['requirements'] })`. Preserve the requirements query's `refetchOnWindowFocus:false` (from `use-runtime-requirements.ts`) so persistently-mounted consumers don't storm the server.

Acceptance criteria (RTL + jsdom, mock Transport):
- Clicking Connect on an install-kind OpenCode triggers the mutation and renders progress.
- On resolve, requirements refetch and OpenCode shows Ready.
- On reject, an honest error renders with a retryable Connect.
- No focus-refetch storm is introduced (requirements stays explicit-refresh).
tasks.activeForm: Wiring the OpenCode Connect CTA to provisioning

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P2] Add the CredentialProvider port, providers config block, and semver migration
tasks.description:
Introduce the T1 credential substrate (ADR-0315): a narrow `CredentialProvider` port plus a `providers` config block whose values are references, never inline plaintext.

Schema (`packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts`): add a top-level `providers` object whose values are credential REFERENCES using a `keychain:<id>` / `env:<VAR>` / `file:<name>` scheme. Extend `runtimes.codex` and `runtimes.opencode` (currently `{ enabled, binaryPath, ... }`) with credential/provider reference fields (e.g. `codex.credentialRef: string | null`, and for OpenCode the selected provider + per-provider refs). Follow the `adding-config-fields` skill end-to-end (Zod field -> `USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS` -> migration -> docs -> tests).

Migration (`apps/server/src/services/core/config-manager.ts`): append `backfillProvidersDefaults` to the `CONFIG_MIGRATIONS` chain keyed to the next ascending release placeholder (the chain currently tops out at `0.47.0` `backfillRuntimesDefaults`; `/system:release` reconciles the concrete version at tag time), mirroring the existing backfills — additive + idempotent, using the `{ get, set }` store shape. The schema default must also yield the `providers` block on read so a fresh config is valid without the migration having run.

Port (`apps/server/src/services/core/credential-provider.ts`): `resolveReference(ref: string): Promise<string | null>` dispatching on scheme — `env:` reads `process.env`; `file:` reads a dork-home-scoped secret file reusing the `ExtensionSecretStore` pattern (`packages/shared/src/extension-secrets.ts` — write-only, never echo, one JSON file under `{dorkHome}/...`); `keychain:` reads the OS keychain, guarding availability and falling back honestly. Secrets are NEVER logged or serialized into `config.json`.

Acceptance criteria (Vitest, tests run against a temp `dorkHome`):
- Reference resolution for each scheme (`keychain:` / `env:` / `file:`), including a DANGLING reference -> resolves `null` with an honest error (surfaced later by Connect UX).
- A secret set via the port never appears in the serialized `config.json` (assert the persisted JSON holds only the reference string, not the value).
- Migration-up: a pre-`providers` config gains the block on load; existing keys untouched (no breaking change). Update `apps/server/src/services/core/__tests__/config-manager.test.ts` and `contributing/configuration.md`.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the CredentialProvider port and providers config block

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P2] Resolve credential references at each runtime's env-injection seam
tasks.description:
Wire the `CredentialProvider` (2.1) into where each runtime assembles its subprocess env, so a stored REFERENCE becomes a real secret in the child env at spawn — without persisting plaintext. Resolution is per-spawn, never cached as plaintext, never logged.

Seams (each runtime SDK stays confined to its adapter dir — hard rule):
- Claude: `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/messaging/message-sender.ts` builds `sdkOptions.env = { ...process.env, CLAUDE_CODE_EMIT_SESSION_STATE_EVENTS: '1' }` (the seam the spec names). Inject a resolved `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` here ONLY when a Claude credential ref is configured; otherwise leave host/delegated-login auth untouched.
- OpenCode: `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/server-manager.ts` spawns `opencode serve` with `OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD` + `OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT`. Add resolved provider keys (e.g. `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, and an optional `baseURL`) to that spawn env.
- Codex: `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/codex/codex-runtime.ts` DELIBERATELY never sets `CodexOptions.env` (the child inherits `process.env`; setting env would drop PATH/HOME/CODEX_HOME — codex/NOTES.md). Do NOT clobber the env. Route Codex auth through the CLI login (2.3) which writes to the child's `CODEX_HOME`, or inject only where the SDK supports a per-call key.

DECISION FLAG (reversible): Codex has no safe full-env override, so API-key injection for Codex relies on the delegated `codex login` (2.3) rather than an env var. Default: inject keys at the safe env seams (Claude, OpenCode); route Codex subscription/API auth through the CLI login. Flag for review.

Acceptance criteria (Vitest, mock CredentialProvider; keep runtime conformance green):
- Claude message-sender includes the resolved key in `sdkOptions.env` ONLY when a ref is configured, and the key value never appears in logs.
- OpenCode server-manager spawn env carries the resolved provider key when configured.
- Codex path does not clobber `process.env` — assert `CodexOptions.env` stays unset.
tasks.activeForm: Resolving credential references at the runtime env seam

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P2] Add connect endpoints: store credential reference + delegate vendor login
tasks.description:
Build the server connect surface (ADR-0318) in a new `apps/server/src/routes/runtimes.ts` mounted at `/api/runtimes` in `apps/server/src/app.ts` (alongside the existing `app.use('/api/...')` block).

Endpoints:
- `POST /api/runtimes/:type/credential`: accepts a secret + target scheme, stores it via the `CredentialProvider` (2.1) as a reference (WRITE-ONLY — the response NEVER echoes the secret, mirroring `apps/server/src/routes/extensions.ts`'s `PUT /:id/secrets/:key`), and updates the runtime's config credential ref (2.1).
- `POST /api/runtimes/:type/login`: spawns the vendor CLI login terminal-free (`claude login` for `claude-code`, `codex login` for `codex`) as a child process and DETECTS completion (poll `codex login status` / a Claude equivalent, or watch the child exit code), returning a completion signal. Bound a hung login with a timeout so it never blocks; loopback-only.

Security: all secret-bearing endpoints reject non-loopback origins and never log secrets. Add transport methods for both in `packages/shared/src/transport.ts` + `HttpTransport`/`DirectTransport`.

Acceptance criteria (Vitest integration, MOCK child_process + CredentialProvider — never spawn a real login in CI):
- Storing a credential persists only a REFERENCE; the response body and logs contain no secret.
- The login endpoint spawns the correct CLI and detects success (child exits 0 / `login status` flips to satisfied) and failure (hung login bounded by a timeout -> honest error, never blocks).
- A non-loopback request is rejected.
tasks.activeForm: Adding connect endpoints for credentials and delegated login

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P2] Build the Codex Connect UI: native API key + delegate codex login
tasks.description:
Codex's terminal-free Connect flow (ADR-0318). In the client connect UI (extend the `apps/client/src/layers/features/runtime-connect/` slice from 1.6, or the entities/runtime setup surface): when Codex is `state:'connect'` with `connect.kind:'login'`, offer two honest choices —
- 'Paste API key' (native in-app input -> `POST /api/runtimes/codex/credential` storing a `CODEX_API_KEY` reference via 2.3; the sanctioned, clean path).
- 'Sign in with ChatGPT' (button -> `POST /api/runtimes/codex/login` delegating `codex login`, with a spinner while awaiting completion detection).

NO reimplemented ChatGPT subscription OAuth (Non-Goal; OpenAI's terms unverified). On success, invalidate `['requirements']` so Codex flips to Ready. Because the vendored binary is already resolved in T0 (1.1), there is NO install step for Codex — the flow is auth-only.

Acceptance criteria (RTL + jsdom, mock Transport; FSD features layer):
- The paste-key path posts the credential and flips Codex to Ready on requirements refetch.
- After submit, the secret is not echoed back into a readable field.
- 'Sign in with ChatGPT' triggers the login mutation, shows progress, and resolves to Ready on completion / shows an honest error on failure.
- No install affordance renders for Codex (binary already resolved).
tasks.activeForm: Building the Codex Connect UI

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P2] Build the Claude Connect UI: delegate claude login + paste key
tasks.description:
Claude's conservative connect (ADR-0318; Non-Goal: never a reimplemented claude.ai browser OAuth). When Claude is `state:'connect'`, offer 'Sign in' (button -> `POST /api/runtimes/claude-code/login` delegating `claude login`, detect completion) or 'Paste API key' (-> store an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` reference via 2.3). Claude stays on delegate-to-host-login. On success, invalidate `['requirements']` so Claude flips to Ready.

Files (client, `apps/client/src/layers/features/runtime-connect/`; FSD features layer). Reuse the same connect mutation + invalidate pattern as Codex (2.4) so the flows are consistent.

Acceptance criteria (RTL + jsdom, mock Transport):
- The delegate-login button triggers the login mutation and flips Claude to Ready on completion.
- Paste-key stores the reference without echoing the secret.
- No claude.ai OAuth UI exists anywhere in the flow (assert only delegate + paste-key affordances render).
tasks.activeForm: Building the Claude Connect UI

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P2] Add OpenRouter connect: paste-key, OAuth-PKCE provisioning, model catalog (server)
tasks.description:
Server support for the OpenCode Gateway (OpenRouter) path (spec §OpenCode provider picker; ADR-0318). Endpoints in `apps/server/src/routes/runtimes.ts`:
- Store an OpenRouter key as a reference via the `CredentialProvider` (2.1) — the always-available paste-key fallback.
- OAuth-PKCE key provisioning: `POST /api/runtimes/opencode/openrouter/oauth/start` generates a `code_verifier` + `state` and returns the authorize URL; a loopback callback `GET /api/runtimes/opencode/openrouter/oauth/callback` validates `state`, exchanges the code for a SCOPED API key, and stores it by reference. This is the one place a fully-native in-app OAuth is unambiguously ToS-clean — OpenRouter is built for app integration.
- `GET /api/runtimes/opencode/openrouter/models` proxies the OpenRouter catalog (cached with a short TTL) to populate the client model dropdown.

Before finalizing, confirm the exact scope/callback contract against OpenRouter's current app-integration docs (spec Open Question); the paste-key path is the fallback if the OAuth contract shifts. Secrets never echoed.

Acceptance criteria (Vitest integration, MOCK the OpenRouter HTTP API — never hit the real provider):
- OAuth-PKCE happy path: start -> callback with matching `state` -> scoped key stored as a reference (not plaintext).
- Denial / `state` mismatch -> honest error, nothing stored.
- Catalog fetch returns options and is cached (a second call within TTL does not re-fetch — assert the mock is hit once).
tasks.activeForm: Adding OpenRouter connect and catalog endpoints

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P2] Add zero-auth Ollama detection (server)
tasks.description:
Detect a local Ollama with zero auth — the frictionless hero of the OpenCode Local path (spec: DorkOS detects, never manages Ollama). NEW `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/ollama.ts` + endpoint `GET /api/runtimes/opencode/ollama` (in `routes/runtimes.ts`) that probes Ollama's local HTTP API at `http://127.0.0.1:11434`: is it running? which models are pulled (`GET /api/tags`)? Use a bounded `fetch` with a short timeout so an unreachable Ollama fails fast and never blocks; cache the result with a short TTL. Return `{ running: boolean; models: Array<{ name: string; size?: number }> }`. When Ollama is running with any pulled coding model, OpenCode's Local path connects with NO account. Do NOT own or manage the Ollama process or its model library — detection only (the guided pull is T2/3.5).

Acceptance criteria (Vitest, mock `fetch`):
- Running + models -> `{ running:true, models:[...] }`.
- Connection refused -> `{ running:false }` and returns fast within the bounded window (assert no hang).
- Malformed / non-JSON response degrades honestly (no throw to the caller).
tasks.activeForm: Adding zero-auth Ollama detection

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P2] Build the OpenCode provider picker UI (Local / Gateway / Direct)
tasks.description:
OpenCode's Connect is 'choose where the model comes from' — build the provider picker (ADR-0318; spec §OpenCode provider picker). Present a small menu with two easy paths featured:
- Local (Ollama) — the zero-auth hero: call `GET /api/runtimes/opencode/ollama` (2.7). If running WITH a coding model, connect with NO account (identity reads 'OpenCode · <model>'); if running WITHOUT a coding model, show a placeholder for the guided pull (delivered in 3.6); if absent, link to the Ollama installer (one-click download, no management).
- Gateway (OpenRouter): 'Paste OpenRouter key' (+ a get-a-key link) storing a reference via 2.3, OR 'Connect OpenRouter' via the OAuth-PKCE flow (2.6); populate the model dropdown from `GET /api/runtimes/opencode/openrouter/models`.
- Direct provider: paste a provider key + optional `baseURL` (same shape as gateway; stored via 2.3).

Files (client): a feature/widget slice under `apps/client/src/layers/features/runtime-connect/` (FSD: features import entities/shared only). On any successful connection, invalidate `['requirements']` so OpenCode flips to Ready.

Acceptance criteria (RTL + jsdom, mock Transport):
- Local path with a detected coding model connects with NO auth input and flips to Ready.
- Gateway paste-key stores a reference and populates the model dropdown from the catalog.
- 'Connect OpenRouter' starts the OAuth flow (navigates to / opens the authorize URL).
- Direct key + `baseURL` stores a reference.
- Ollama-absent shows the installer link, not an error.
- FSD import-direction clean.
tasks.activeForm: Building the OpenCode provider picker UI

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P3] Show identity as runtime + model everywhere
tasks.description:
Surface identity as runtime AND model ('OpenCode · qwen2.5-coder'), not runtime alone (spec decision 8), making the local-model superpower legible. Extend the client identity surfaces:
- `apps/client/src/layers/entities/runtime/ui/RuntimeMark.tsx` and the status chip `apps/client/src/layers/features/status/ui/RuntimeItem.tsx` + `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/status/use-runtime-chip.ts`: render the session's model beside the runtime label, resolved via `useCapabilitiesForRuntime` (`apps/client/src/layers/entities/runtime/model/use-runtime-capabilities.ts`) + the session's selected model. OpenCode surfaces `provider/model` (e.g. via `projectModelOptions` in `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/models.ts`, which encodes `provider/model` and a human `provider · model` description).
- Where no model is selected yet, render the runtime alone (honest — no invented model).
- Keep `runtime-descriptors.ts` (`getRuntimeDescriptor`) the single source of visual identity; do not fork icon/label logic.

Acceptance criteria (RTL + jsdom, mock Transport):
- A started OpenCode session with model `ollama/qwen2.5-coder` renders 'OpenCode · qwen2.5-coder'.
- A runtime with no selected model renders just the runtime label.
- The mark is consistent across the chip and the session list (assert both render the same identity string).
tasks.activeForm: Showing identity as runtime plus model

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P3] Add "Run this with…" re-run into a fresh session
tasks.description:
Add 'Run this with…' — re-run the current prompt into a FRESH session bound to another runtime (ADR-0255 immutable per-session binding: a switch is ALWAYS a new session, never a mutation and never a history transplant; the cross-runtime history-transplant Non-Goal is preserved from DOR-180).

Hook the existing prompt + `?runtime=` launch path:
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-session-submit.ts` (first-turn runtime hint), the `runtime` param read by `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/status/use-runtime-chip.ts`, and `apps/client/src/router.tsx`'s `/session` `search.runtime`.
- Add an affordance (e.g. on a user prompt or `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/status/ChatStatusSection.tsx`) that opens a small runtime menu reusing `getRuntimeDescriptor` + the Ready/Connect gating from T0 (so an unconnected target routes to Connect FIRST via `useRuntimeReadiness`), then navigates to `/session` with `dir` = the SAME cwd, `runtime` = the chosen type, and the prompt pre-filled/auto-sent into the new session.
- Never carry the prior transcript.

Acceptance criteria (RTL + jsdom, mock Transport + router):
- Choosing 'Run this with Codex' from a Claude session navigates to a NEW `/session?...&runtime=codex` with the prompt seeded and NO prior messages transplanted.
- A not-yet-connected target opens Connect instead of launching.
- The original session is untouched (assert its transcript is unchanged).
tasks.activeForm: Adding Run-this-with re-run into a fresh session

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P3] Add per-model nature badges (privacy/cost + honest capability)
tasks.description:
Add per-model nature badges at the point of choice so the real tradeoff (privacy/cost vs frontier capability) is visible, not hidden (Rams; spec decision 11). In the provider picker (2.8) and model surfaces, render badges: 'local · private · free' (Ollama / OpenAI-compatible local providers) vs 'cloud · per-token' (OpenRouter / direct cloud), PLUS an honest capability signal. Add a short benefit line on the Local path — 'Private and free: runs on your machine; your code never leaves it; no per-token bills' — with the plain counterweight that frontier quality still comes from Claude/Codex or a top cloud model via OpenRouter.

Honesty rule (DOR-180 research): tool-calling below ~14B is unreliable — never badge a small (e.g. 7B) model as Claude-equivalent. Derive the nature from the model's provider/id (local provider ids -> local; else cloud).

New entities/runtime UI, e.g. `apps/client/src/layers/entities/runtime/ui/ModelNatureBadge.tsx` (FSD: entities). Add a Dev Playground showcase (per the `maintaining-dev-playground` skill) so all badge states are visually testable.

Acceptance criteria (RTL + jsdom):
- An Ollama model renders the 'local · private · free' badge + benefit line.
- An OpenRouter / cloud model renders 'cloud · per-token'.
- A sub-14B local model does NOT claim frontier-equivalence (assert the honest capability caveat renders).
tasks.activeForm: Adding per-model nature badges

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P3] Build the dedicated Runtimes surface (widget + route)
tasks.description:
Build the dedicated Runtimes surface — the home for discovery + connect (spec §Runtimes surface). NEW widget `apps/client/src/layers/widgets/runtimes/` and a route in `apps/client/src/router.tsx` at `/runtimes` (mirror the `/marketplace` route + `widgets/marketplace` pattern: `createRoute` under `appShellRoute`, added to the route tree, plus a nav entry). Renders three sibling cards (Claude, Codex, OpenCode), each showing Ready or a single Connect, composing:
- the T1 connect flows (Codex 2.4, Claude 2.5, OpenCode provider picker 2.8),
- the nature badges (3.3),
- identity = runtime + model (3.1).

This is where a Claude-only user DISCOVERS DorkOS speaks Codex and OpenCode. Responsive across mobile/tablet/desktop (quality standard). FSD: widgets may import features/entities/shared.

Acceptance criteria (RTL + jsdom, mock Transport + router):
- The surface lists all three runtimes as siblings with correct Ready/Connect states derived from the requirements projection.
- Clicking Connect on each runtime opens its respective flow.
- The `/runtimes` route is reachable and deep-linkable.
- FSD import-direction clean; add a Playground/e2e smoke where feasible.
tasks.activeForm: Building the dedicated Runtimes surface

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P3] Add the guided Ollama pull endpoint + hardware heuristic (server)
tasks.description:
Server side of the guided, hardware-aware Ollama model pull (spec decision 6, T2). Extend `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/opencode/ollama.ts` (2.7):
- `POST /api/runtimes/opencode/ollama/pull` triggers a single Ollama pull (`POST http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/pull` for a curated coding model, e.g. `qwen2.5-coder`) and STREAMS download progress (Ollama's pull API streams `status`/`completed`/`total` lines).
- A STATIC hardware heuristic (resolved Open Question: lean static first) estimating 'runs well' vs 'may be slow' from available VRAM/RAM vs the model's size, with honest sizing copy either way (e.g. '~18 GB'). Keep the heuristic small and pure so it is unit-testable.

Boundary: DorkOS only detects + triggers a pull and (at most) links to the Ollama installer; it NEVER owns Ollama's process or model library.

Acceptance criteria (Vitest, MOCK the Ollama HTTP API — never hit a real Ollama in CI):
- The pull streams progress frames and completes.
- A failed pull (non-2xx / stream error) degrades to an honest error.
- The heuristic returns a 'runs well' / 'may be slow' verdict for representative (VRAM, model-size) inputs (table-test a few pairs).
tasks.activeForm: Adding the guided Ollama pull endpoint and hardware heuristic

tasks.subject: [effortless-runtime-switching] [P3] Build the guided Ollama pull UI in the provider picker (client)
tasks.description:
Client side of the guided pull, inside the OpenCode Local path of the provider picker (2.8). When Ollama is running but NO coding model is pulled, offer a curated one-click pull (`qwen2.5-coder`) with:
- the hardware-aware sizing verdict from 3.5 ('~18 GB; runs well / may be slow on your hardware'),
- streamed download progress (`motion` progress per `contributing/animations.md`).

On completion, connect OpenCode to the newly pulled local model (zero auth) and flip to Ready with identity 'OpenCode · qwen2.5-coder' (using 3.1). If Ollama is absent, keep the installer link (no management). Honest sizing — never oversell a small model (reuse the honest-capability caveat from 3.3).

Acceptance criteria (RTL + jsdom, mock Transport):
- With Ollama running and no coding model, the guided-pull CTA appears with the sizing verdict.
- Triggering it streams progress and, on completion, connects to the local model and shows Ready with the runtime+model identity.
- A pull failure surfaces an honest, retryable error.
- Ollama-absent shows the installer link, not the pull CTA.
tasks.activeForm: Building the guided Ollama pull UI
